title: Run optimizer for Medication Review Prescriber

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Preparing a fresh optimizer run for Medication Review: Prescriber using the direct CLI path. Requested parameters: 12 cycles, 180-day feedback window, max 100 samples, starting from current champion 27816b39-0bb8-4fd8-adec-934d40cb47ab.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Launched a new direct-CLI optimizer run for Medication Review: Prescriber. Procedure ID: adf366f9-4e0e-415b-8ab0-a2a203e6b805. Starting version: 27816b39-0bb8-4fd8-adec-934d40cb47ab. Parameters: 180-day feedback window, max 100 samples, max 12 iterations.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: The first optimizer attempt failed in setup, not in the scoring logic. Exact error from the direct CLI run: plexus_score_pull returned SCORE_NOT_FOUND after 3 attempts. Root cause reproduced directly: plexus_score_pull fails when given scorecard/score names but succeeds with the scorecard ID 59125ba2-670c-4aa5-b796-c2085cf38a0c and score ID 00c72712-4651-47d3-b32f-2f8904540b2c. Relaunching the optimizer with IDs as a workaround.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Retry launched with scorecard/score IDs instead of names to work around the procedure-time score_pull resolver bug. New procedure: ad648a45-6bd3-4c38-838c-5ba44694a377. New task: 130041a7-0c0d-4d94-9e99-12e3824fa97a.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Retry with scorecard/score IDs successfully bypassed the initial procedure-time score_pull name-resolution bug. The retried optimizer still failed, but for a different reason: the baseline regression evaluation crashed because Medication Review: Prescriber returned GraphRecursionError on specific feedback items during scoring. Example failures surfaced in the direct CLI log: item 309504413 / feedback item 43709332-f4e0-4338-b3e1-e59121b47c92 and item 306082262 / feedback item 5aaa24b3-f672-49a0-a331-a93b72632ca2. This is now a score/runtime execution bug, not the original procedure resolver bug.

comments.text: Restarted Prescriber optimizer from patched local code. New procedure: 538c4ab5-40e9-463a-8b63-95aba9382f37. New task: 75e81bd3-c235-4830-a016-7f5ccc302686. Launch params: 180-day feedback window, max_samples=100, max_iterations=12, start_version=27816b39-0bb8-4fd8-adec-934d40cb47ab. Monitoring the direct CLI run instead of relying on dashboard state alone.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Killed local optimizer process 91247 for procedure 538c4ab5-40e9-463a-8b63-95aba9382f37 because the Prescriber champion baseline is mechanically unstable due to empty/partial LLM completions. Fixing the live score before any further optimizer runs.

Inspecting local PIDs with ps and comparing against procedure timeout-stale dry-run output to determine whether any older Medication Review: Prescriber run is still local and killable or only stale in persisted task/procedure state.

comments.author: ryan

Expanding the local process search beyond direct procedure optimize/run commands to confirm whether any local worker/dispatcher process is driving it or whether the run is only active in persisted state on another host.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Found the lingering older local run. It is not a procedure optimize process; it is a score optimize wrapper process: PID 98688 (). That elapsed time matches the older Prescriber optimizer launch. Confirming its child PIDs before sending TERM to that exact process.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Found and terminated the lingering older local optimizer wrapper for Medication Review Prescriber. It was not a procedure optimize process; it was a score optimize wrapper at PID 98688 with the older 12-cycle parameters. TERM did not stop it, so it required kill -9. The surviving local optimizer process is the newer 4-cycle procedure optimize run at PID 11372.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Reviewing the post-invalidation Medication Review: Prescriber evaluation bd50de24-c17f-4daa-8d1a-ddc86c6e353b to identify which remaining misclassifications still look like the old rubric class we already corrected versus which ones reflect real remaining score/rubric issues. Goal: connect the current error set back to the recent Prescriber rubric clarification and feedback invalidation work.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Reviewed evaluation bd50de24-c17f-4daa-8d1a-ddc86c6e353b (Medication Review: Prescriber, score version 99aa28ab-268d-47c1-80d7-ab3e7c7d4b7c) after the first Prescriber invalidation batch. Important finding: the evaluation is still contaminated by stale labels because it contains at least three explicitly invalidated Prescriber feedback items from the approved batch. Confirmed stale items in this evaluation: 306181806 / feedback 71febae1-9a45-4d58-a19b-bf31d810ca58 (Yes->No misclassification), 305252079 / feedback 9f1f8fab-0a02-42d0-893a-697625b3cdf7 (No->No), and 305141717 / feedback f2d731f8-f411-4d30-b896-3a05f28c6f7a (No->No). So this evaluation is not a clean post-invalidation measure yet; it likely used a dataset snapshot built before those invalidations. Aside from the stale-label contamination, the remaining error mix is mostly implementation/mechanical, not rubric ambiguity: evaluation RCA reports 7 score_configuration_problem, 4 mechanical_malfunction, 0 guideline_gap_requires_sme, 0 information_gap. Main remaining themes: undercounting unresolved meds on some Yes->No/No->Yes boundary calls, RelevantWindowsTranscriptFilter removing prescriber-verification evidence on several false negatives, and missing-required-context / truncated processed-input problems on several others.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Reviewed evaluation bd50de24-c17f-4daa-8d1a-ddc86c6e353b after the first Prescriber invalidation batch. This is not a clean post-invalidation read yet: the evaluation still contains at least three explicitly invalidated Prescriber feedback items (306181806 as a Yes->No miss, plus 305252079 and 305141717 in No/No). Among the 11 actual misclassifications, RCA reports 7 score_configuration_problem and 4 mechanical_malfunction, with 0 guideline_gap_requires_sme and 0 information_gap. Remaining false negatives cluster around RelevantWindowsTranscriptFilter evidence loss / missing required context, while the two non-stale false positives look like real unresolved-med counting disagreements rather than the old 'not all meds' rubric class.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Confirmed likely root cause for stale invalidated labels in Prescriber evaluation: contradiction report path explicitly excludes isInvalid feedback items, but evaluation-related fetch/build paths do not. FeedbackEvaluation._fetch_feedback_items in plexus/Evaluation.py fetches FeedbackItems without requesting or filtering isInvalid. FeedbackService.find_feedback_items also does not request/filter isInvalid. Associated dataset curation fetches isInvalid but _is_qualifying_feedback_item ignores it, so curated datasets can still materialize invalidated feedback. This explains why the fresh contradictions report dropped the invalidated Prescriber items while evaluation bd50de24-c17f-4daa-8d1a-ddc86c6e353b still contained them.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation of invalidated-feedback exclusion across optimizer-relevant ingestion paths: Evaluation feedback fetch, FeedbackService, FeedbackItems data cache, and dataset curation eligibility. Will add/extend tests and run targeted test suite.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented invalidated-feedback exclusion across optimizer-relevant ingestion paths (FeedbackEvaluation._fetch_feedback_items, FeedbackService.find_feedback_items, FeedbackItems._fetch_feedback_items_for_scores, and dataset curation eligibility in _is_qualifying_feedback_item). Added tests covering all four paths and ran targeted suite: /Users/ryan/miniconda3/bin/python -m pytest -q plexus/cli/feedback/feedback_service_test.py plexus/cli/dataset/curation_test.py plexus/Evaluation_feedback_test.py plexus/data/test_feedback_items.py -> 69 passed. Also ran live integration spot checks on Medication Review: Prescriber (180 days): FeedbackEvaluation fetch, FeedbackService fetch, and collect_qualifying_feedback_items each returned zero intersection with invalidated IDs {71feb...,9f1f...,f2d7...,e3c7...,fb01...}.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented and pushed invalidated-feedback exclusion fix across optimizer-relevant ingestion and curation paths. Commit: a1acc17cc. Targeted tests pass (69). Live spot checks for Medication Review: Prescriber show zero intersection with known invalidated feedback IDs in feedback evaluation fetch, FeedbackService fetch, and curation qualifying set.
